title: Sabicea ceylanica
what_description: Sabicea ceylanica is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a shrub or small tree that grows up to 3 meters in height, with a stem diameter of 1-2 centimeters. The leaves are opposite, elliptic, and 4-7 centimeters long, with a rounded apex and a cuneate base, and are covered with soft, dense hairs on both surfaces. The flowers are white, with a 5-lobed calyx and a corolla with 4-5 lobes, and are arranged in axillary cymes. The plant is endemic to Sri Lanka and is found in the wet zones of the country, typically in forests and grasslands.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Sabicea ceylanica are diabetes, hypertension, and skin infections, which it treats by reducing blood sugar levels, lowering blood pressure, and inhibiting the growth of pathogens. Additionally, it is used to alleviate inflammation, arthritis, and fever through its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. Sabicea ceylanica is also effective in treating respiratory issues, such as asthma, and gastroenteritis, while its antimalarial properties help combat malaria. Furthermore, it is utilized to heal wounds, gastritis, and skin ulcers, while also treating dysentery and eczema. Lastly, it is also used to alleviate rheumatism, high blood pressure, menstrual pain, and cancer through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Sabicea ceylanica are its antimicrobial activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins that inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, thereby preventing infections. This plant also possesses antioxidant properties, due to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids that scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Moreover, Sabicea ceylanica exhibits anti-inflammatory activity, with sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids contributing to the reduction of inflammation and alleviation of pain. Additionally, the analgesic properties of this plant are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and alkaloids that interact with pain receptors, providing relief from pain. Furthermore, the antifungal activity of Sabicea ceylanica is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and saponins that inhibit the growth of pathogenic fungi.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Sabicea ceylanica are quercetin, flavonoids, kaempferol, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. Quercetin, a potent flavonoid, has been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties, while flavonoids have been found to exhibit neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects. Kaempferol, a flavonoid glycoside, has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer, and antioxidant activities. Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, while alkaloids, including sabiceanin and corynoxine, have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. These bioactive compounds contribute to the medicinal properties of Sabicea ceylanica, making it a potential candidate for the treatment of various diseases.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Sabicea ceylanica are derived from its leaves and include sabicea infusion, a liquid extract made by steeping the leaves in hot water, sabicea tincture, a concentrated liquid made by soaking the leaves in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin, sabicea tea, a liquid made by steeping the leaves in hot water, sabicea decoction, a liquid made by boiling the leaves in water, and sabicea salve, a topical ointment made by infusing the leaves in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. These preparations are typically used to treat various health issues due to the plant's antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Sabicea ceylanica has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The different preparations allow for varying levels of bioavailability and absorption of the plant's compounds, making them suitable for different uses. Overall, the herbal preparations of Sabicea ceylanica offer a range of potential health benefits.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Sabicea ceylanica improperly include experiencing nausea, which may be due to its high alkaloid content, specifically saponins, that can irritate the stomach lining and cause vomiting. Ingestion of excessive amounts can cause diarrhea, a result of the plant's laxative properties, leading to an imbalance of electrolytes and dehydration. Developing allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing, is also a possible side effect, as some individuals may be sensitive to the plant's bioactive compounds. The improper use of Sabicea ceylanica can induce nausea, potentially leading to further complications, such as electrolyte imbalances and organ damage. Furthermore, using the plant inappropriately can lead to severe health consequences, including organ failure and even death, especially in cases of long-term consumption or high doses.
uses: Sabicea ceylanica is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Sri Lanka. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been used to treat fever, colds, and other respiratory issues. Its leaves and bark are used in the preparation of herbal remedies. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
taxonomy: Sabicea ceylanica is classified under the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It belongs to the genus Sabicea, which consists of various species of flowering plants native to tropical regions. The classification of Sabicea ceylanica is further divided into its kingdom, phylum, class, and order as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Magnoliophyta, class - Magnoliopsida, and order - Gentianales. The family Rubiaceae is a member of the clade asterids within the larger clade angiosperms. Sabicea ceylanica's classification provides essential information about its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
benefits: Sabicea ceylanica, a plant species native to Sri Lanka, is said to have medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems. Its leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Sabicea ceylanica for medicinal use.
parts: Sabicea ceylanica is a plant species native to Sri Lanka.  It is known for its potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its uses.
